methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $15M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 25.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Atomic Semi is a Seed-stage semiconductor company founded by Sam Zeloof and Jim Keller that has raised $15M+. The valuation assessment draws on a comp universe of 8 private peers in chips and semiconductors at similar or adjacent stages, alongside 3 public semiconductor comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD), though the latter cannot be directly applied here.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-Progression analysis applies a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement, using the $15M raised as a proxy baseline, yielding $38M. Peer-Median analysis across four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—Etched ($5.0B Series A), d-Matrix ($2.0B Series A), Substrate ($1.0B Series A), and Rain AI ($500M Series A)—produces a median valuation of $1.5B, with a range spanning $500M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (sector median 25.3x) cannot be applied, as the target's revenue is not disclosed and public multiples rarely translate to early-stage private companies.

The triangulated range is $32M (low) to $1.7B (high), with a median of $769M. This wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty: the Stage-Progression method anchors conservatively on disclosed capital, while Peer-Median captures the substantial valuations achieved by later-stage frontier-chip entrants. Resolution depends on validating whether Atomic Semi's technical position and market opportunity align more closely with early-stage risk or with the proven traction evident in Series A peers.
